skill.name: longbridge-portfolio
skill.description: Analyze Longbridge account assets, positions, cash flows, statements, margin data, orders, DCA plans, and portfolio risk using Longbridge CLI workflows.
skill.summary: Review Longbridge portfolio data, order workflows, risk metrics, and allocation plans.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many backtick, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and one URL finding, but reviewed examples are markdown documentation and finance-analysis text rather than executable code. The real risk is intentional use of Longbridge external commands that can access account data and mutate brokerage state, so publication should include a clear medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Mutating Brokerage Command Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally covers order buy, sell, cancel, replace, and DCA create, update, pause, resume, and stop workflows. This is legitimate for a brokerage skill, but mistakes can affect real money, so the built-in preview and explicit-confirmation requirements are security-critical.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files directly describe mutating brokerage operations and mandatory confirmation controls. This is not malicious, but the financial impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Access To Account Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands for assets, positions, portfolio data, price history, and risk calculations. This is expected behavior, but it exposes sensitive account information to the active assistant session and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple documented workflows require external Longbridge CLI calls that read account and market data. The commands are purposeful and bounded, but they are still elevated-risk integrations.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in markdown inline code, fenced examples, command names, and output templates. I did not find evidence that these backticks are executable Ruby code inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected files are markdown references, and the cited backticks delimit documentation text or shell examples. No Ruby source file or runtime execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Finance Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography static findings did not correspond to cryptographic API usage in the reviewed contexts. The cited files discuss cash-flow fields, DCA confirmation steps, benchmark deviation, or skill metadata, not hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static locations are markdown prose and command guidance. I did not find calls to MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic primitives.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Account Workflow Text
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance findings are tied to parameter checks, plan lookup guidance, and portfolio-analysis wording. They do not instruct host enumeration or local system discovery beyond expected Longbridge account lookup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are financial workflow instructions and analysis descriptions. No evidence of host, user, process, credential, or filesystem reconnaissance was found there.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is An Install Reference
low_findings.description: The single hardcoded URL points to the longbridge-terminal GitHub project in an error-handling row. It is a documentation link, not a hidden exfiltration endpoint or unauthorized network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible in markdown as an installation reference. There is no code path that sends account data to that URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Mutating CLI Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes documented command patterns for trade and recurring-investment mutations. These are dangerous if confirmation controls are skipped, although the skill explicitly requires preview and confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command names are explicit mutating brokerage operations. The surrounding text mitigates risk through confirmation requirements but does not remove the financial impact.

content.value_statement: Investors need a clear way to review account holdings, cash flow, risk, and order activity. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge CLI workflows with confirmation steps for trading actions.
actual_capabilities: Routes portfolio questions to focused reference files for assets, positions, cash flow, statements, margin, and orders.
Documents Longbridge CLI commands for account assets, stock positions, fund positions, P&L, and cash-flow review.
Provides portfolio analysis frameworks for diagnosis, rebalancing, allocation, risk, attribution, and tax-loss harvesting.
Requires preview and explicit user confirmation before order or DCA mutating commands.
Supports Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English responses based on user input.
limitations: Most account workflows require an authenticated Longbridge session and the correct account permissions.
Order and DCA actions can affect real brokerage accounts and must not run without explicit confirmation.
Analysis quality depends on available Longbridge data and current CLI behavior.
Portfolio outputs are informational and are not financial, tax, or investment advice.

use_cases.title: Review Account Holdings
use_cases.description: Summarize current Longbridge assets, stock positions, fund positions, buying power, and floating P&L in the user language.

use_cases.title: Prepare Risk Reports
use_cases.description: Use Longbridge holdings and price history to estimate concentration, correlation, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, and stress scenarios.

use_cases.title: Control Trading Workflows
use_cases.description: Preview order or DCA changes, confirm exact parameters, and execute only after the user gives explicit approval.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize My Portfolio
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Longbridge portfolio summary with total assets, cash, positions, and major P&L drivers. Use my input language.

prompt_templates.title: Check Buying Power
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my available cash, buying power, margin ratio, and maximum buy quantity for the symbol I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Portfolio Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Longbridge holdings for concentration, currency exposure, sector mix, correlation risk, and historical drawdown risk.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Confirmed DCA Action
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a preview for a Longbridge DCA plan using my symbol, amount, frequency, and dates. Do not execute until I explicitly confirm.

output_examples.input: What are the main risks in my Longbridge portfolio?
output_examples.output: A concise risk report with concentration level, largest holdings, currency exposure, high-correlation pairs, historical drawdown estimate, and clear caveats.

output_examples.input: Create a monthly DCA plan for AAPL.US after I provide the amount and date.
output_examples.output: A preview listing symbol, amount, currency, frequency, start date, end condition, and a request for explicit confirmation before execution.

output_examples.input: Review my cash-flow records for the last quarter.
output_examples.output: A summary of deposits, withdrawals, dividends, settlements, currencies, dates, and notable account cash-flow changes.

best_practices: Use JSON output from the Longbridge CLI when summarizing account data for the model.
Verify current CLI help before commands that involve money, orders, or recurring plans.
State data sources, analysis date, assumptions, and investment-advice limitations in portfolio reports.
anti_patterns: Do not execute order, cancel, replace, or DCA commands without a separate explicit confirmation.
Do not guess missing amount, currency, frequency, day, symbol, or plan identifiers.
Do not present historical risk estimates as predictions or personalized investment advice.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents trading workflows, but mutating commands require a preview and explicit user confirmation first.

faq.question: Which Longbridge permissions are needed?
faq.answer: Public margin and quantity checks may not need login. Account data, orders, statements, and DCA workflows usually require Trade permission.

faq.question: Can it analyze portfolio risk?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for concentration, correlation, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, performance attribution, and stress-test reports.

faq.question: Can it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It can organize data and calculations, but outputs must remain informational and include appropriate caveats.

faq.question: What languages does it support?
faq.answer: The skill asks the assistant to match Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English based on the user input.

faq.question: What happens if the CLI is unavailable?
faq.answer: The references tell the assistant to install longbridge-terminal or use MCP fallback when available, without bypassing confirmation rules.
